# Cross-Chapter Synthesis — Regulatory Sanctions (GOOD: multi-file parallel audit)

This synthesis was produced after a multi-file parallel audit loaded Chapter A
and Chapter B simultaneously. The audit surfaced a cross-chapter inconsistency
in the characterisation of Decree No. 47's sanction mechanism, which was
resolved against the primary decree text before finalising.

## Effect Inventory — Decree No. 47 (2019)

- Sanction type: **suspension** (not immediate revocation)
- Effective date: 90 days after formal recording of the second failing cycle
- Response window: institutions may submit a remediation plan within 90 days
- Reinstatement: requires a successful follow-up audit within 24 months

## Synthesis

Decree No. 47 (2019) imposes a structured suspension regime. Both Chapter A
and Chapter B now reflect the unified effect inventory above. Institutions face
a 90-day suspension window — not immediate revocation — giving resource-
constrained institutions meaningful time to prepare a remediation response.

     simultaneously, surfacing the cross-chapter inconsistency (suspension vs
     immediate revocation) that a sequential file-by-file audit would miss.
     The effect inventory above reconciles both chapters against Decree No. 47's
     primary text. This is the correct D1 behaviour: parallel sweep first,
     unified effect inventory before synthesis. -->
